name: Looking for Ms. Keaton
description: This week-long showcase celebrates the career and craft of Diane Keaton, exploring her legacy as a paradigm-shifting performer and auteur.

name: Umber Majeed: JY TECH
description: This exhibition by Umber Majeed explores the intersection of digital technology, history, and the South Asian diaspora.

name: Abang-guard: Makibaka
description: A contemporary exhibition by the artist collective Abang-guard, exploring themes of resistance and collective action through various media.

name: Spectrum by Queen Andrea
description: View a vibrant and energetic large-scale public art installation designed by artist Queen Andrea.

name: El Brunch at Oyamel
description: Savor a Mexican-inspired brunch featuring specialty dishes and cocktails at Oyamel by José Andrés.

name: CoffeeRight: Creator Gallery
description: This gallery features a diverse collection of artists whose work is inspired by New York City.

name: Happy Hour at Quin Bar
description: Enjoy daily happy hour specials on signature cocktails and snacks at the stylish Quin Bar.

name: Joan Jonas: Moving off the Land II (Excerpts)
description: Columbia University School of the Arts presents excerpts of work by illustrious alum Joan Jonas '65 as part of its yearlong 60th anniversary celebration.

name: About a Living Culture
description: Nepalese artist IMAGINE (Sneha Shrestha) presents a temporary public art installation featuring an arch made of repeating rows of 'Ka,' the first letter of the Nepali alphabet. The sculpture invites New Yorkers into a space of reflection and belonging in the heart of Jackson Heights.

name: CONTEXT: Art, Books, & Freedom
description: This exhibition features 15 artists whose work incorporates books and text, exploring themes of social commentary, political statement, and literary allusion. The artists utilize books and text as tools of investigation, pushing the boundaries of free expression within the context of a liberal arts library.

name: Chiharu Shiota: Two Home Countries
description: This is the first New York solo museum exhibition of Berlin-based contemporary artist Chiharu Shiota, featuring her signature thread installations.

name: Athi-Patra Ruga: Lord, I gotta keep on (movin’)
description: This multidisciplinary exhibition centers on myths, power, and liberation through performance, film, painting, textile, and glass. Artist Athi-Patra Ruga creates allegorical figures that explore Xhosa history and post-apartheid South Africa.

name: Soft Spaces
description: Soft Spaces is an exhibition exploring queer themes and artistic expression through a variety of contemporary works. The show is part of the museum's radical focus on queer and trans artists.

name: Fia Backström: The Great Society
description: An expansive exhibition examining the legacy of "The Great Society" through multi-media works and contemporary perspectives on social structures.

name: Recent Works by Bjorn Amelan
description: Multitalented artist Bjorn Amelan presents new sumi and acrylic ink paintings on handwoven hemp canvases.

name: David Wojnarowicz: Arthur Rimbaud in New York
description: A series of photo-performances created between 1978 and 1979 documenting the artist’s state of mind and the urban decay of New York City. The works feature subjects wearing a mask of poet Arthur Rimbaud in various symbolic locations.

name: New Sunday Roast at queensyard
description: Enjoy a traditional British Sunday roast featuring roasted meats and seasonal sides in a sophisticated setting.

name: Of the Earth: Connections
description: Part of the Indigenous Practice Studio, this exhibition explores the profound relationships between community, land, and cultural practice.

name: Mazorca Colectiva Entre Semilla y Fibra: Threads of Memory, Seeds of Land
description: This exhibition focuses on reclaiming ancestral memory through seed-growing and fiber practices guided by community elders.
short_name: Threads of Memory, Seeds of Land

name: Ruth Asawa: A Retrospective
description: A retrospective of Ruth Asawa's work is being held at MoMA. The exhibition showcases a comprehensive look at her career.

name: The Met: Live in HD 2025 Through 2026
description: The Peabody- and Emmy Award-winning series returns, broadcasting live operatic performances from the Metropolitan Opera in high definition.
short_name: The Met: Live in HD 2025 Through 2026

name: Nicole Eisenman STY
description: Exhibition of Nicole Eisenman's work. The exhibition is located at 52 Walker Street in New York.

name: Notice/Know/Kin: A Human/Tree Biography
description: A mixed-media art exhibit by Nina Browne that invites visitors to reflect on our living relationships with trees and the Earth. The works reveal wood as a dynamic, resilient, and reciprocal living entity.

name: Visualizing Legacies and Futures: An Exhibition
description: This exhibition reflects the historical and contemporary memory of the National Black Theatre through rare archival photography and original film footage. It explores the preservation of Black theater history as a radical act of reclamation and resistance.

name: Legacy Lab: Life Interwoven
description: This exhibition features the works of artists Lola Flash and groana melendez in dialogue with Bronx community family archives.

name: Jeff Koons: Porcelain Series
description: This exhibition is the first dedicated presentation of Jeff Koons's Porcelain series, featuring sculptures made of mirror-polished stainless steel. The works are modeled on eighteenth- to early twentieth-century figurines and characters from classical mythology.

name: Pinkmas at Edge
description: Experience a whimsical, pink-themed holiday wonderland set against breathtaking panoramic skyline views.

name: Live Performances at Shine Bright
description: Watch live musical and seasonal performances staged amidst the "Shine Bright" holiday light display.

name: The Anatomy of a Movie Poster: The Work of Dawn Baillie
description: A pop-up exhibition of original movie posters in partnership with Poster House, celebrating the iconic career of Dawn Baillie.

name: Champagne Pommery Sparkles
description: Enjoy festive champagne moments and seasonal bubbly offerings from Champagne Pommery.

name: Bronx Community Board 8 Meetings
description: Bronx Community Board 8 meetings are held via Zoom. Details on how to join can be found on the website.

name: December '25 - Committees Agendas
description: This link provides access to the agendas for the committee meetings in December 2025. It's a resource for understanding the topics to be discussed.

name: Art in Focus
description: Rockefeller Center presents a series of public art pop-ups inspired by the New York City landscape and contemporary life. These installations can be found in unique and unexpected locations across the Center.

name: Rockefeller Center Christmas Tree
description: The Rockefeller Center Christmas Tree will be on view nightly from its lighting on December 3rd until mid-January 2026. It is a central attraction for holiday celebrations in New York City.

name: Migrant Housing: Water as a Medium for Healing
description: An exhibition of work by BFA Interior Design students, alumni, and a collaborative group, exploring water as a medium for healing in migrant housing.
short_name: Water as a Medium for Healing

name: Peppermint Flavors at Van Leeuwen
description: Celebrate the holiday season with limited-time peppermint-inspired ice cream flavors and gift options.

name: fables for introverts, chapter iii: limbo
description: An exhibition of recent paintings by Gerald Euhon Sheffield II, exploring landscapes, American folklore, national identity, and personal reflection.

name: End of Season Sale at Kate Spade
description: Shop for discounts on designer handbags, accessories, and clothing during this end-of-season retail event.

name: Winterscapes by Wells Fargo
description: Explore immersive holiday-themed landscapes and festive floral displays throughout the shopping center.

name: NYC Parks’ 43rd Annual Wreath Interpretations
description: This festive tradition welcomes the holiday season with nearly 40 wreaths made from unique and unusual materials.

name: The Gotham All-Stars
description: A professional showcase featuring comics from various popular television shows and films.

name: BIPOC Climb Night
description: Join us for Black, Indigenous, People of Color Climb Night at LIC to climb and be a part of the community! These evenings are hosted by the BIPOC Climbing Collective.

name: An Unmarried Woman
description: Mazursky’s smash comedic drama stars a breakthrough Jill Clayburgh as a well-to-do Manhattan woman whose snake of a husband (Michael Murphy) leaves her, without warning, for a younger woman after sixteen years of marriage.

name: The New York Sari
description: This exhibition showcases the diverse and evolving tradition of the sari in New York. It explores how this garment has been adapted and worn by different communities in the city.

name: The Gay Harlem Renaissance
description: This exhibition illuminates the vibrant LGBTQ+ contributions to the Harlem Renaissance. It highlights the intersection of art, culture, and identity during this pivotal era.

name: Declaring the Revolution: America’s Printed Path to Independence
description: This exhibition traces the pivotal role of print in shaping the American Revolution. It examines how pamphlets, newspapers, and broadsides fueled the movement for independence.

name: Holiday Express: Toys and Trains from the Jerni Collection
description: This festive exhibition showcases a delightful collection of vintage toys and trains. It offers a nostalgic journey through holiday traditions and childhood memories.
short_name: Toys and Trains from the Jerni Collection

name: Stirring the Melting Pot: Photographs from The New York Historical Collections
description: This exhibition presents a compelling collection of photographs documenting the diverse immigrant experience in New York. It captures the city's evolving identity through the lens of its residents.
short_name: Photographs from The New York Historical Collections

name: The Recordings: Voices from the _Shoah_ Tapes
description: This exhibition features oral histories from Holocaust survivors, preserving their powerful testimonies. It offers a profound and personal look at one of history's most tragic events.

name: Saturday Sensory Hour
description: Every Saturday from 10am - 11am, we dim our lights and quiet our exhibits so that visitors can have an all around less stimulating experience here before it gets crowded.

name: Deep Disco
description: Join us for DEEP DISCO, a night of soulful grooves and electronic beats. Get ready to dance the night away!

name: Poetry and Performance Series
description: Poets, spoken word performers, and vocalists activate Rashid Johnson’s Rotunda Stage in a series of weekly performances.

name: Architecture Tour
description: Join us on the rotunda floor to learn more about the Guggenheim’s iconic architecture. 30 minutes, free with museum admission.

name: Soup & Soul
description: Enjoy a cozy afternoon with delicious soup and soulful music. A perfect way to relax and unwind.

name: La Ternura
description: A comedy following three women and three men as they navigate the peculiar realities of love and relationships on a desert island. The play features hilarious mix-ups and magical misunderstandings.

name: Sanguine: Musical Activations
description: Experience weekly musical activations of Rashid Johnson’s Sanguine, a site-specific installation featuring local performers and special guests.

name: Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee
description: Please arrive no earlier than 6:45PM for the meeting. Space is limited. Agenda for the meeting can be found here. If you wish to view the meeting virtually, you may watch the livestream on our YouTube channel by clicking here.

name: Environmental Protection Committee
description: Please arrive no earlier than 6:45PM for the meeting. Space is limited. Agenda for the meeting can be found here. If you wish to view the meeting virtually, you may watch the livestream on our YouTube channel by clicking here.

name: Budget & Governmental Affairs Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. The agenda includes discussion of energy cost legislation and the Open Meetings Law.

name: ladies night
description: A monthly meetup for women, nonbinary individuals, and those who feel at home in women-centered spaces. All skill levels are welcome to climb and connect in a supportive, LGBTQIA+ affirming environment.

name: Blues Jam Session
description: Immerse yourself in the raw emotion and improvisation of a live blues jam session. All musicians are welcome to join!

name: Permits and Licenses Committee Meeting
description: This meeting will focus on reviewing liquor license and cannabis retail license applications. Notices with specific establishments will be finalized on December 2nd.

name: Executive Committee
description: Please arrive no earlier than 6:45PM for the meeting. Space is limited. Agenda for the meeting can be found here. If you wish to view the meeting virtually, you may watch the livestream on our YouTube channel by clicking here.

name: Blue Pig Test – Series Event
description: A recurring comedy event series.

name: Youth & Education Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. A presentation on NYC education policy will be given, along with a discussion of the "Freedom to Read Act."

name: CRUX Meetup
description: A climbing meetup for Trans, Non-binary, and Intersex individuals, fostering empowerment through rock climbing. Rentals are included, and no sign-up is necessary.

name: Queens Community Board 4 Monthly Meeting
location: Please call the office of Community Board 4 to verify meeting dates and locations.
description: Boards meet once each month to address community concerns. A portion of each meeting is reserved for public input.

name: La Breve y Maravillosa Vida de Oscar Wao
description: This play explores the endless human capacity to persevere in the name of love, following Oscar, a young Dominican-American man dealing with a family curse. It's a humorous and poignant performance.
short_name: La Breve y Maravillosa Vida de Oscar Wao

name: Event
description: This is a placeholder event.

name: Grand Rounds
description: This event features a lecture with a title and speaker to be announced, focusing on medical topics. The lecture will be followed by a Q&A session and can be joined in person or via Zoom.

name: Full Board Meeting
location: Lewis Davis Pavilion at 25 Waterside Plaza
description: This is a hybrid meeting where the public can speak on community issues. In-person attendance is limited, so Zoom is recommended.

name: Weekly ParaCliffHangers Meetup
description: ParaCliffHangers strive to make climbing accessible to all while building community and fostering personal empowerment. Join ParaCliffHangers for weekly meetups every Wednesday night where all levels are welcome.

name: Civic/Public Safety/Religious + Health/Human Service/Senior Citizens/Veterans Committee Meeting
description: The agenda includes a presentation on NYC Smoke-Free Brooklyn Initiatives and Programs. Further topics are to be confirmed.
short_name: Civic/Public Safety/Religious + Health/Human Service/Senior Citizens/Veterans Committee Meeting

name: General Board Meeting
description: Agenda for the meeting can be found here. If you wish to view the meeting virtually, you may watch the livestream on our YouTube channel by clicking here.

name: Health & Environment Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. Topics include Bellevue Hospital's budget priorities and a discussion to prohibit nonessential helicopter flights.

name: Unik Fridays at Amadeus Nightclub
description: Kick off your weekend with Unik Fridays at Amadeus Nightclub. Enjoy a unique atmosphere with great music and an energetic crowd to start your night off right.

name: Tumi Semi-Annual Sale
description: Find premium discounts on high-quality luggage, backpacks, and travel accessories during the semi-annual sale.

name: Joan Semmel: In the Flesh
description: Semmel, a defining force of feminist art, has mined the Jewish Museum’s collection to present a series of works by other artists in dialogue with her own.

name: Asian Climbing Tribe
description: These meetups will be facilitated by Asian Climbing Tribe leaders and feature rotating partner vendors every month. Asian Climbing Tribe’s mission is to provide a safe space for members of the Asian community.

name: Latin Night
description: Spice up your Friday night with a vibrant Latin party. Dance to salsa, bachata, reggaeton, and more!

name: 30th Poets House Showcase
description: Celebrate the 30th Poets House Showcase, a literary exhibition and reading series of poetry from 2024 and 2025.

name: Housing & Homelessness Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. The agenda includes discussion of the Faith-Based Affordable Housing Act and a housing survey.

name: Sunday Brunch Vibes
description: Kick off your Sunday with a delightful brunch accompanied by smooth, laid-back tunes. A perfect start to your day.

name: Artechouse Holiday Special
description: Step inside Spectacular Factory, a digital artistry world where lights dance and a fantastical holiday workshop comes to life.

name: Brooklyn Community Board 7 Meeting
description: This is a monthly meeting for Brooklyn Community Board 7. Details about the agenda will be posted closer to the meeting date.

name: Monthly Board Meeting
description: This is the monthly board meeting for Brooklyn Community Board 18, open to the public and conducted via Webex. Details for joining the meeting are provided, including links and access codes.

name: Full Board Meeting (Public Hearing and Regular Meeting)
description: This is the regular monthly meeting for the Full Board, which includes a public hearing and a regular meeting. Public comment will be accepted.

name: Executive Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. The agenda includes the Chair's Report, discussion of the CB6 email policy, and review of upcoming committee agendas.

name: Fluid and Flux
description: A group exhibition featuring the Fall 2025 Artist Residency Project cohort navigating themes of identity, memory, and perception.

name: Business Affairs & Licensing Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. The agenda is still being formed, but will include the Chair's Report and old/new business.

name: Bipoc Meetup
description: Black, Indigenous, People of Color Climb Night is an opportunity to climb and build community. Hosted by the BIPOC Climbing Collective, it aims to create more inclusive spaces in climbing.

name: Pop Forum _I Wonder About Us Folks_
description: Exhibition featuring Pop Forum. _Holiday Hours:_ December 25 – Closed December 26-27 & 30, 12-6pm December 31 & January 1, Closed
short_name: Pop Forum _I Wonder About Us Folks_

name: Techno Thursday
description: Experience the driving rhythms and hypnotic sounds of techno music. A night for true electronic music enthusiasts.

name: CRUX Newbie Night
description: Join us on the third Friday of every month for CRUX Newbie Night! Designed for new or less-experienced climbers, CRUX hosts and teaches climbers about climbing equipment, safety, and technique.

name: Disco Dance Off
description: Get your boogie on at our Disco Dance Off! Show off your best moves to classic disco hits.

name: Jazz Lounge
description: Unwind in our Jazz Lounge with smooth melodies and sophisticated vibes. Enjoy a night of live jazz performances.

name: District Office Committee Meeting
description: This meeting will cover Board Member Attendance Review and District Office Reports.

name: Land Use & Waterfront Meeting
description: This meeting will cover land use and waterfront topics. The agenda is TBD.

name: Reggae Sunday
description: Soak up the good vibes with an evening of reggae music. A chill and uplifting way to end your weekend.

name: Happy New Year (ends Dec 31)
description: Ring in the New Year with a special event that runs through the end of the year.

name: After Work Rooftop Party
description: End the work week with a rooftop celebration featuring drinks and panoramic city views.

name: 230 Fifth Fridays
description: Dance the night away at Manhattan's largest rooftop garden and penthouse lounge.

name: 54 Sings Broadway's Greatest Hits!
description: Scott Siegel gathers the very best voices from Broadway to perform a well-thought-out collection of some of the best songs from musical theater history.

name: Got To Be There: The Apollo, Its People, and Its Stories
description: This immersive exhibition explores the history of the world-famous Apollo Theater, celebrating its people and stories.
sublocation: Stages at The Victoria - Laura and Frank Baker Gallery
short_name: The Apollo, Its People, and Its Stories

name: Oscar Noriega's Crooked Quartet
description: Composer and multi-instrumentalist Oscar Noriega performs a mixture of standards and original compositions for alto saxophone and clarinet.

name: Recycle Your Tree
description: Help Battery Park City stay green by depositing your holiday tree without decorations at the curb. Trees are chipped and used for mulch in local parks.

name: The World Famous Harlem Gospel Choir
description: Experience the powerful voices and uplifting energy of New York's premier gospel group during their signature brunch series.
short_name: The World Famous Harlem Gospel Choir

name: Stand Up Comedy In New York City.
description: Top headliners seen on Netflix, HBO, and Comedy Central perform in the heart of the theater district.
short_name: Stand Up Comedy In New York City.

name: Just in Time
description: A high-spirited jukebox bio-musical starring Jonathan Groff that explores the life and music of legendary performer Bobby Darin. The production transforms the venue into a swanky nightclub for an intimate theater-going experience.

name: Joan Jonas: Moving off the Land II (Excerpts)
description: This exhibition features eleven large-scale drawings of red fish by alumna Joan Jonas, exploring the science and stories of the world's oceans. The drawings are digitally scanned and installed as banners for the School of the Arts 60th anniversary.

name: CONTEXT: Art, Books, & Freedom
description: Curated by Meg Hitchcock, this exhibition brings together 15 artists who use books and text as tools of political and social investigation. The works explore the boundaries of expression and the psychological impact of visual language.

name: George Balanchine's The Nutcracker®
description: Experience the magic of the holiday season with a 40-foot Christmas tree and swirling snow in the Land of Sweets. This production features the Sugarplum Fairy and delectable characters in a classic holiday tradition.

name: Carlos Henriquez Nonet
description: Bassist and composer Carlos Henriquez blends jazz with Afro-Caribbean traditions to perform music from his latest album, A Nuyorican Tale.

name: Father Mother Sister Brother
description: Jim Jarmusch’s triptych film exploring familial dynamics through three chapters set in rural New Jersey, Dublin, and Paris.

name: The Secret Agent
description: A thrillingly unpredictable, shape-shifting epic set in 1970s Recife following a man on the run from his past during Brazil's military dictatorship.

name: Holiday House Tour
description: Experience yuletide through the ages with decorated historic interiors representing the 1820s and 1860s. The tour concludes in the Courtroom which features a 15-foot Christmas tree.

name: Dirty Circus
description: House of Yes presents its longest-running variety show featuring circus, cabaret, burlesque, and aerial acrobatics. The night is told in three acts and is not for the faint of heart.

name: Resurrection
description: In a future where humans cannot dream, an outcast finds nightmarish visions and beauty in an intoxicating world of his own making. A work of staggering imagination from director Bi Gan.

name: Train Dreams
description: Based on the novella by Denis Johnson, this film follows Robert Grainier as his life unfolds in an era of unprecedented change in the American Pacific Northwest.

name: Twin Peaks: Season 2, Ep. 1
description: Agent Cooper has a vision of a giant who gives him clues about the murder of Laura Palmer. Screening as part of the series Wonderful and Strange: The Complete Twin Peaks.

name: Twin Peaks: Season 2, Ep. 2 & 3
description: Part of the "Wonderful and Strange" series, these screenings include episodes "Coma" and "The Man Behind the Glass" from the iconic mystery series.

name: Twin Peaks: Season 2, Ep. 4 & 5
description: Screenings of the episodes "Laura's Secret Diary" and "The Orchid's Curse" as part of a complete retrospective of the series.

name: Twin Peaks: Season 2, Ep. 6 & 7
description: Cooper and the One-Armed Man inspect guests at the Great Northern in these pivotal Season 2 episodes, "Demons" and "Lonely Souls."

name: Oldies Happy Hour
description: Enjoy happy hour at Oldies featuring Japanese shochu and craft cocktails starting at $6.

name: Fort Hamilton Distillery Tour
description: Explore the world of craft distilling and New York’s whiskey heritage with an immersive tour and spirits tasting.

name: Holiday Adventure
description: Celebrate the season with live animal shows featuring penguins and kangaroos, a mini train ride, and a sock skating rink.

name: Amahl and the Night Visitors
description: This holiday family opera follows a young boy visited by three mysterious kings on a journey to visit a newborn child. It is the first seasonal family offering in Lincoln Center Theater history.

name: Ragtime
description: A sweeping musical adaptation of E.L. Doctorow’s novel that follows three fictional families in pursuit of the American Dream. The production features a lush score by Stephen Flaherty and Lynn Ahrens on the grand Vivian Beaumont stage.

name: Bug
description: A sexy psychological thriller about romance, paranoia, and conspiracy in a motel room. Reality slips out of grasp as two broken people face a dangerous connection.

name: Wine & Cheese Crash Course
description: Join a Murray’s expert for an interactive crash course on wine and cheese pairings. You will taste your way through delicious cheeses and perfect beverage pairings.

name: Designing Motherhood: Things that Make and Break Our Births
description: This exhibition explores the arc of human reproduction through the lens of design, focusing on the objects and environments that shape the experience of birth. It examines how design can both facilitate and hinder these fundamental human experiences.
short_name: Things that Make and Break Our Births

name: Douriean Fletcher: Jewelry of the Afrofuture
description: Fletcher presents bold jewelry designs that blend ancient aesthetics with Afrofuturist concepts, celebrating cultural identity and wearable art. The exhibition features pieces that combine traditional African aesthetics with futuristic concepts.

name: The Mad MAD World of Jonathan Adler
description: A major exhibition celebrating the prolific career of designer Jonathan Adler, showcasing his signature pottery and "Modern American Glamour" aesthetic. It features a wide array of his ceramics, furniture, and textile designs.
short_name: The Mad MAD World of Jonathan Adler

name: Dana Barnes: Untamed Gestures
description: This exhibition features the large-scale textile and fiber works of Dana Barnes, exploring the relationship between material, space, and tactile form. The works utilize raw materials and physical processes to create immersive environments.

name: Before-Hours Tours with an Art Historian
description: Join a professional art historian for an exclusive early morning tour of the galleries before the museum opens to the public.

name: You Are Here
location: Museum of the City of New York
description: This exhibition draws on a rich archive of movies set in New York, combining cinematic moments across 16 screens. It provides an immersive look at how the city is portrayed on film.

name: The Stettheimer Dollhouse in a New Light
location: Museum of the City of New York
description: The beloved Stettheimer Dollhouse returns to the galleries following an extensive conservation project. It is a highlight of the museum's collection showcasing intricate miniatures and history.
short_name: The Stettheimer Dollhouse in a New Light

name: Songs of New York
location: Museum of the City of New York
description: This kinetic, immersive experience introduces visitors to a full range of music from and about New York City. It features playful and interactive elements full of surprises.

name: Urban Stomp
location: Museum of the City of New York
description: Immerse yourself in the vibrant dances that have shaped and been shaped by the city's cultural landscape. The exhibition celebrates the movement and rhythm of New York.

name: Holiday Train Show
description: See model trains navigate meticulously crafted plant-based replicas of NYC landmarks in the Enid A. Haupt Conservatory. This year features new models of the Delacorte Theater and the Whitney Museum of American Art.

name: Evergreen Express
description: Children can explore a treetop walk, create mini evergreen swags to take home, and play with outdoor train toys. The adventure also features a winter-themed Story Trail based on the book "Bear and Wolf."

name: Holiday Train Nights
description: Experience the Holiday Train Show after dark with live music, seasonal cocktails, and illuminated displays. These adult-friendly celebrations feature roving conductors and sweet or savory bites for purchase.

name: The Strangers Project
description: An interactive display where you can read thousands of anonymous personal stories or write and share your own.

name: Rewind by Whisbe
description: A contemporary installation by WhIsBe featuring oversized childhood toy sculptures that explore innocence and resilience.

name: Marty Supreme
description: A film screening of the sports-themed drama starring Timothée Chalamet, reportedly inspired by a professional table tennis player.

name: Song Sung Blue
description: A romantic drama inspired by the true story of a Neil Diamond tribute band and their journey through life and love.

name: Momix Alice
description: Tumble into a dazzling world of illusion and imagination with MOMIX’s ALICE, a breathtaking adventure through Wonderland filled with acrobatics and holiday whimsy.

name: Beatriz Santiago Muñoz: El Ombligo del Sueño
description: A screening of Beatriz Santiago Muñoz's work El Ombligo del Sueño, featuring 16mm film transferred to digital video. This six-minute piece explores visual textures and stereo sound.

name: Happy New Year (ends Dec 31)
description: Join the nightly celebration leading up to the end of the year at this Upper West Side speakeasy.

name: Youth Activist Meeting
description: A monthly gathering for young activists to lead the fight for safe streets and sustainable transportation across the five boroughs.

name: Hip Hop Night
description: Celebrate the culture of hip hop with live DJs spinning classic and new tracks. Get ready to move!

name: Saturday Sunset
description: Enjoy stunning skyline views as the sun sets over the city at this weekly rooftop gathering.

name: 230 Fifth Saturdays
description: Experience the ultimate weekend party with live DJs and incredible views of the Empire State Building.

name: Bill Carney's Jug Addicts
description: A raucous ensemble performing a repertoire of jug band, old time, tin pan alley, and early jazz and blues.

name: Live Operator Hours: Featuring Devon Turnbull in the Listening Room
description: Join artist Devon Turnbull as he activates an immersive sound installation with music selected for his handmade high-fidelity audio system.
short_name: Featuring Devon Turnbull in the Listening Room

name: Fitness at the Fort
description: A free, year-round exercise program led by Nancy Bruning that incorporates climbing staircases, stretching, and forest bathing.

name: Tailored Industry November Sale
description: Shop over 300 knitwear products marked down to 50% off at the brand's new retail store location.

name: CREA Drop Off Adventure
description: A 60-minute immersive experience for kids that combines technology with traditional play in a sensory-rich environment.

name: Rye Revolution Distillery Tour
description: Learn the history of Rye whiskey and the craft of distilling while tasting four whiskeys and spirits straight from the barrel.

name: Wine & Spirits Tastings Every Saturday!
description: Join Sahadi Spirits for weekly wine or spirit tastings to discover new favorites before the year ends.

name: Standard Wormwood Live Music
description: Enjoy live music from a rotating lineup of genres accompanied by craft cocktails and snacks at the distillery.

name: Burrata Making
description: Learn the art of cheese making in this hands-on double feature class focused on stretching mozzarella and filling burrata. An expert will guide you through the process as you create and taste fresh cheeses.

name: Member Early Hour
description: Museum members enjoy exclusive early access to the museum's galleries before they open to the general public.

name: Winter Wonderland Tree Tour
description: Embark on an invigorating 45-minute guided walk to view the Garden’s stately conifer collection and old-growth forest. Discover the beauty of these collections even in the cold winter months.

name: Winter Composting with Big Reuse
description: Join the NYC Compost Project with Big Reuse to manage the 5-bin compost system at Randall's Island Urban Farm. Volunteers will help process yard waste and food scraps to create soil amendment for local green spaces.

name: Sounds of Bollywood: Desi Saturdays
description: Mix and mingle at this glamorous weekly Bollywood night featuring high-energy music and a packed house. Top DJs spin the finest Desi beats for a high-society party experience.

name: Print Your Own Stationery
description: Work with designers to produce your own custom set of stationery by setting type, mixing ink, and using a 19th-century printing press.

name: Holiday Tea
description: Enjoy a festive and elegant afternoon tea service featuring seasonal treats and a variety of teas.

name: Open for cocktails
description: Join Zinc Bar for an unforgettable night of signature cocktails and great vibes in a unique atmosphere.

name: Ice Skating
description: Skate on New York City's largest free-admission ice rink in the heart of Midtown. Reservations are required for all skaters, including those bringing their own skates.

name: Le Carrousel
description: Take a ride on the park's charming carousel featuring a variety of animals and traditional French cabaret music. It is a whimsical attraction designed for children and families.

name: New York City One-Shot: Talon Bar
description: Join a Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition one-shot adventure where all materials, including pre-made character sheets, are provided. Players of all experience levels are welcome to learn and play in a social setting.
short_name: New York City One-Shot: Talon Bar

name: Happy Hour Jazz
description: A weekly Saturday night jazz jam session featuring local musicians with no cover charge.

name: Open Mic (Free w/ $5 Bar Purchase!)

short_name: New Edition The New Edition Way Tour

name: Frank El Flaco “¿Quién Dijo Miedo?”
description: Frank El Flaco performs his newest stand-up special for a night of Spanish-language laughs.

name: HANABIE. - North America Tour 2026
description: Japanese metalcore sensation HANABIE. brings their high-energy Harajuku-core sound to Brooklyn.

name: St. Lucia
description: Synth-pop group St. Lucia performs two full sets, showcasing their upbeat melodies and tropical vibes. This 16+ show is a celebration of their expansive catalog.

name: Brooklyn Guitar School Student Showcase

description: Artists Siobhan McBride and Lea Simoniello discuss their creative practices and the themes behind their Triennial works. The talk covers topics ranging from slippery memories to excavations of the psychological landscape.

name: Moondoggy

name: Artemas
description: Emerging pop talent Artemas performs his infectious hits alongside guest Isabel LaRosa. This is a 16 and over concert.

name: Charla en la sala: Lectura de un edificio (Spanish language)

name: Filthy Rotten System
description: An activist protest rock band performing a repertoire ranging from Woody Guthrie and Pete Seeger to powerful original social commentary.

name: Jeff Mills
description: An evening with "The Wizard," a true pioneer of the Detroit techno sound and a master of the 909 drum machine. Jeff Mills offers a transcendental journey through space-inspired electronic music.

name: Designing with Type Ornaments: A Weekend of Printmaking Play
description: Explore the visual potential of vintage type ornaments and modular forms using letterpress equipment.

name: Paper Decorations: Part 1/2
description: Learn traditional and contemporary techniques for creating decorative paper for use in book arts.

name: St. Patrick's Open Day 2026
description: The 26th annual free open house featuring traditional music, dance, language lessons, film screenings, and crafts for all ages.

name: Fintan O’Toole and Sam McBride

name: Kaylee Rose
description: Country-pop artist Kaylee Rose performs live on a Saturday night.

name: Celebrating Sabicas
description: A tribute to the legendary flamenco guitarist Sabicas, featuring world-renowned musicians and dancers.

name: NYC Art Bazaar

name: Contemporary Music Festival

name: Three Italian Tenors

description: Acclaimed pianist Jeremy Denk performs a solo recital featuring his insightful interpretations of classical repertoire.

name: Bryce Crawford I Love Jesus U.S. Tour

name: Brendan James “I Got a Song to Sing” Tour

short_name: Brendan James “I Got a Song to Sing” Tour

name: The Reading Series: Sycamore

name: How My Grandparents Fell in Love, A Musical

name: Martin Hayes and the Common Ground Ensemble

name: G.B.a.T.P.M.O.a.P.F.T.S.I.T.M.T.T.

name: Mary Beth Barone: Galaxy Brain

name: Swanky By Sindhu Vee

name: The Reading Series: creation stories and all the important importants

name: Melanin Madness Film Screening

description: Rapper CupcakKe brings her high-energy, unapologetic, and provocative performance style to the Brooklyn stage. This concert is open to fans 16 and over.

name: Claude McKAY: From Harlem to Marseille
description: A screening and Q&A session focused on the life and work of Jamaican-American writer Claude McKay. The film traces his journey through the Harlem Renaissance and his wanderings in Europe.
short_name: Claude McKAY: From Harlem to Marseille

name: Chinary Ung
description: Grawemeyer Award-winning composer Chinary Ung combines Cambodian folk traditions with contemporary classical techniques in this career-spanning Portrait. The Del Sol String Quartet performs several of his works, including a new world premiere commission.

name: Spider Rabbit
description: An experimental production featuring surrealist puppetry and avant-garde performance elements.

name: Milo Rau & Édouard Louis: The Interrogation
location: NYU Skirball Center for the Performing Arts
description: A raw, intimate collaboration blending personal testimony with urgent political critique, revisiting moments of violence and class shame in France.

name: Rain Garden Stewardship Event
description: Join the NYC DEP Green Infrastructure team to learn gardening basics and how to become a steward for local rain gardens.

name: The Westerlies Play Bill Frisell: Album Release Show
description: This brass quartet premieres never-before-heard compositions from Bill Frisell's recent notebooks and highlights from his catalog.

name: Tony Trischka In Residence: Great Big World
description: Banjoist Tony Trischka performs a wide-ranging program exploring diverse musical landscapes and global influences.

name: Calla
description: A raw Butoh performance exploring the tension between fragility and resilience through slow, deliberate movement.

name: Eliane Elias - The Art of Bossa Nova
description: Multi-Grammy Award winner Eliane Elias brings the smooth sounds of Brazil to NYC with her special "Art of Bossa Nova" performance.
short_name: Eliane Elias - The Art of Bossa Nova

name: Teacher Workshop | Colors of the World
description: A professional development workshop for educators exploring global artistic traditions, pigment history, and cultural palettes.
short_name: Teacher Workshop | Colors of the World

name: Rob Osler: The Case of the Murdered Muckraker
description: Rob Osler visits the shop to discuss The Case of the Murdered Muckraker.
short_name: The Case of the Murdered Muckraker

name: Shabaka
description: The celebrated saxophonist and composer Shabaka Hutchings presents his latest musical explorations. The performance spans jazz, African heritage, and contemporary experimental sounds.

name: STEM Night: Women in STEM
description: A virtual event for teens and young adults highlighting the career journeys and professional advice of women working across various STEM fields.

name: Clem Burke - Rock ‘n’ Roll Memorial & Life Celebration
description: A special memorial and celebration of life for Clem Burke, featuring live music and tributes. Musicians and fans gather to honor a significant legacy in the rock world.
short_name: Clem Burke - Rock ‘n’ Roll Memorial & Life Celebration

name: Israel On Our Minds – Session Two
description: Ambassador Ido Aharoni continues his monthly update series on Israeli affairs, security, and global criticism. This session provides insider analysis of the rapidly shifting reality in the Middle East.
short_name: Israel On Our Minds – Session Two

name: Working Together in Theatre - With Joel Veenstra
description: Joel Veenstra shares insights on professional collaboration and the interpersonal dynamics of working in the theatrical industry.

name: Mae Martin: The Possum
description: Mae Martin performs their latest stand-up hour, "The Possum," blending comedy with personal observation.

name: Lecture Series - Glass in Context Part II: The Rise of the Designer
description: Jane Bruce examines the role of the designer in glass history from the 19th to the 21st century in this educational series session.

name: Gogol Bordello
description: The legendary gypsy punk band brings their chaotic and infectious energy for a high-octane live show. Expect a riotous blend of Eastern European folk, punk rock, and theatrical performance.

name: Enigma Variations & Bruch’s Violin Concerto
description: Nikolaj Szeps-Znaider leads Bruch’s Violin Concerto No. 1 and Elgar’s legendary Enigma Variations.

name: oskar med k: Make Me Feel Tour 2026
description: Oskar med k brings his signature electronic and house-inspired sound to a late-night slot as part of his 2026 world tour. The event offers a modern club atmosphere with driving dance beats.
short_name: oskar med k: Make Me Feel Tour 2026

name: Philharmonic Families: Winds
description: An interactive and relaxed performance introducing families to the woodwind family of the orchestra.

name: Paper Decorations: Part 2/2
description: Continuation of the decorative paper series, focusing on advanced patterns and surface treatments.

name: Féile na Gaeilge / Irish Language Day 2026
description: A day-long celebration of the Irish language featuring story-focused workshops, conversation circles, and short films.
short_name: Féile na Gaeilge / Irish Language Day 2026

name: Gearrscannáin na Gaeilge / Irish Language Shorts
description: A screening of contemporary Irish language short films across various genres including comedy, drama, and history.
short_name: Gearrscannáin na Gaeilge / Irish Language Shorts

name: Seanchoíche
description: A storytelling night where volunteer speakers share personal narratives and poetry in the Irish language on the theme of "Home."

name: The Music of Taylor Swift: A Symphonic Tribute
description: A full orchestra performs symphonic arrangements of Taylor Swift's greatest hits from throughout her career.

name: Old 97's
description: Alt-country pioneers Old 97's return to the stage with support from artist Lizzie No. The band is celebrated for their energetic blend of power pop and traditional country roots.

name: Nidia Góngora: Afro-Colombian Music
description: Experience the soulful currents of the Colombian Pacific coast with Latin Grammy-nominated singer Nidia Góngora.

name: Israeli Chamber Project
description: An ensemble of award-winning Israeli musicians performs a program of classical and contemporary chamber works.

name: Bughouse - Closing Performance
description: The final scheduled performance of the world-premiere production of Bughouse at Vineyard Theatre. The play offers an intimate examination of a self-taught artist’s compulsion to create.

name: BRIGHTCAST In-store Playday
description: Meet Nick Forker and Hugh Kramer, the creators of Brightcast, the brand. Join for a playday.

name: Tana French celebrates THE KEEPER
description: Acclaimed mystery author Tana French celebrates her book The Keeper as part of the Midday Mystery virtual series. This event features a live discussion with the author.
